metrics.reason: available at notice 39940000.00 vs redemption demand 50000000.00 = coverage 0.7988 (< 1.0)
liquidity buffer 3000000.00 vs additional margin 4000000.00 = coverage 0.75 (< 1.0)
portfolio liquidation cost 41.0 bps vs watch 75.0 bps
0.3140 of NAV not liquidatable within 30 days (watch 0.2)

caveats: ADV and spread inputs are point-in-time estimates; realized liquidity may differ
the participation-of-ADV model assumes orderly trading and ignores second-order price impact
redemptions and market stress may be correlated across positions and investors
scenario parameters are assumptions, not forecasts; vary them before drawing conclusions
netting, credit lines, and lines of last resort outside the modeled buffer are not counted
disclaimer: Liquidity analysis and evidence only under stated scenario assumptions; not an investment, trading, or fund-liquidity-action determination. No trade, redemption gate, or other liquidity action has been taken.
narrative: Under the transparent stress scenario 'severe-redemption-2026Q3' (ADV haircut 0.50, spreads x2.0, 10% price shock, 50% redemption over a 7-day notice), portfolio AM-BALANCED-01 shows a redemption-coverage shortfall (coverage 0.80 vs demand 50,000,000), a full-liquidation horizon of ~500 days driven by three positions beyond the 30-day cap, and a collateral buffer that covers only 0.75x the modeled additional margin. About 31% of NAV is not liquidatable within 30 days. Suggested liquidity-risk band: Stressed. These are evidenced metrics under stated assumptions for the liquidity risk committee and portfolio manager to weigh alongside the modeling caveats; vary the scenario before drawing conclusions. Liquidity analysis and evidence only under stated scenario assumptions; not an investment, trading, or fund-liquidity-action determination. No trade, redemption gate, or other liquidity action has been taken.
notes: Cost metric (41 bps) is below the 75 bps watch threshold and did not breach; reported for context.
